---
title: "One Day at a Time"
format: html
---

---
title: "One Day at a Time"
format: html
---

# One Day at a Time

Thinking too far into the future can trigger overwhelm (“I can’t do this forever”).The one day at a time approach reduces that pressure by narrowing your focus to **what you can control right now**.

This is NOT avoidance. It is a skillful shift in focus. Instead of solving long-term sobriety all at once, you build it through small, repeatable daily actions.

You don’t have to stay sober forever today. You only have to follow today’s plan.

Over time, these daily commitments become the foundation of long-term recovery.

## Create a daily commitment card to focus your energy on one manageable day, with clear and realistic commitments.

::: callout
### Daily Commitment Card

Today, I will:

**1. Avoid (known risks)**

-   What will I stay away from today?

**2. Do (support recovery)**

-   What is one positive action I will take?

**3. Contact (connection)**

-   Who will I check in with?

**4. Coping Plan (if urges rise)**

-   If I feel an urge, I will:

**5. Complete this sentence:**

- “Today, I only need to \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_.”
:::



![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/sunrise.jpg?raw=true){fig-align="center"}
